entities.name: LAND DEGRADATION
entities.description: The process of land deterioration reaching ten to twelve million hectares per year

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE LAND MANAGEMENT
entities.description: Resource-efficient practices used to maintain land health and resilience

entities.name: RESTORATION
entities.description: Initiatives to recover degraded land with restoration potential

entities.name: REHABILITATION
entities.description: Initiatives to return degraded land to a functional state

entities.name: DEVELOPING COUNTRIES
entities.description: Regions where a significant portion of degraded land is located

entities.name: POVERTY
entities.description: A socio-economic state that sustainable land management aims to alleviate

entities.name: FOOD AND WATER SUPPLIES
entities.description: Essential resources that need to be secured through land management

entities.name: NATURAL RESOURCE BASE
entities.description: The environmental foundation that requires protection

entities.name: STAKEHOLDERS
entities.description: Individuals or groups at local, national, and international levels involved in selecting management options

entities.name: LAND MANAGERS
entities.description: Individuals responsible for implementing land management practices

entities.name: PUBLIC AND PRIVATE SECTORS
entities.description: Economic and governmental entities providing incentives for land management

entities.name: LAND DEGRADATION NEUTRALITY
entities.description: A state where the amount and quality of land resources necessary to support ecosystem functions are maintained or enhanced
